claims.claim_text: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed to US/Israel-linked vessels
claims.notes: High geopolitical significance. Closure would have major economic impact. Source: @CBSNews. | China's statement that 'Our ships are moving in and out of the waters of the Strait of Hormuz. We have trade & energy agreements with Iran. Iran controls the Strait of Hormuz, and it is open for us' directly contests the claim that the Strait is effectively closed. | Web search confirmed the critical importance of the Strait of Hormuz and explicitly mentioned 'False Claims and Denials' regarding activities there, including Iran denying imposing tolls and calling such claims disinformation. This reinforces the 'contested' status and highlights the role of disinformation. | Web search confirms a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is contributing to fuel shortages, adding weight to the 'effectively closed' side for some entities, but China's statement still contests this for others. | The Philippines seeking US permission to buy Russian crude due to fuel shortages and a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz suggests the Strait is effectively closed for some, but China's prior statement still contests this for others. This reinforces the 'contested' status.

claims.claim_text: Closure of Strait of Hormuz caused US gas prices to surpass $4/gallon
claims.notes: Causal claim linking Hormuz closure to gas prices. Correlation may not equal causation.

The @AssalRad post further frames sanctions as 'economic terrorism'.
